hypothesis: Among Si–X alloy anodes identified as having stable Li⁺ migration channel sizes across lithiation states, the specific identity of the alloying element X will determine a quantifiable threshold channel radius (Rt*) below which electrochemical cycling stability degrades precipitously, such that alloys maintaining Rt consistently above this element-specific threshold will exhibit cycle life improvements of at least twofold compared to those with equivalent average Rt but higher variance, a relationship testable by correlating CHGNet-computed per-cycle channel radius distributions against experimental capacity retention curves across a systematic series of Si–X binary anodes spanning groups 13–15 of the periodic table.
